name: Area Formulas — Triangle, Parallelogram and Circle
whatIsIt: Beyond rectangles, each shape has an area formula. RECTANGLE: length × breadth. PARALLELOGRAM: base × height (the straight height, not the slanted side). TRIANGLE: ½ × base × height (half a rectangle). CIRCLE: π × radius × radius (π r², with π ≈ 3.14). Area is always in SQUARE units.
whyNeeded: Real jobs need these: paint for a triangular gable, turf for a round lawn, cloth for a slanted banner. The formulas turn measuring the space inside any common shape into a quick calculation.
realLifeUses: Turf or water for a circular pond (π r²)
Cloth for a triangular flag (½ base × height)
Paint for a triangular wall gable

vocabulary.meaning: The base is a side; the height is the straight (perpendicular) distance to it.
The area of a circle: π times the radius squared.
The unit for area (sq cm, sq m).

story.title: The Triangular Flag
story.text: Neha sews a triangular flag with base 20 cm and height 15 cm. 'How much cloth?' asks the teacher. Neha recalls a triangle is half a rectangle: ½ × 20 × 15 = ½ × 300 = 150. 'One hundred and fifty square centimetres!' The teacher smiles — 'the half is what makes it a triangle.'
story.extractedProblem: A triangular flag has base 20 cm and height 15 cm. What is its area?
story.answerInStory: 150 sq cm — ½ × base × height = ½ × 20 × 15.

visual.caption: A triangle is half of a rectangle with the same base and height → area = ½ × base × height.

teachingGallery.title: One formula per shape
teachingGallery.note: Use the straight height; area is in square units.

standardMethod.summary: Pick the shape's formula, use the perpendicular height (not slant), calculate, and give the answer in square units.
steps: Parallelogram: base × height (the straight, perpendicular height).
Circle: π × radius × radius (π ≈ 3.14). Answer in SQUARE units.

workedExamples.problem: Find the area of a triangle with base 10 cm and height 6 cm.
Find the area of a circle with radius 7 cm (π ≈ 3.14).

commonMistakes.mistake: Using base × height for a triangle (forgetting the ½).
commonMistakes.fix: A triangle is HALF a rectangle: area = ½ × base × height. Don't skip the half.

commonMistakes.mistake: Using the slanted side instead of the straight (perpendicular) height.
commonMistakes.fix: Height means the STRAIGHT distance from the base to the top, at a right angle — not the slanted side.

challenge.q: Circle radius 10 — area (π ≈ 3.14, sq units)? (write like 314)
Ravi finds a triangle's area as base × height. What's the fix?

options.label: Multiply by ½ — it's half a rectangle

challenge.answer: Multiply by ½ — it's half a rectangle

questions.q: The area of a circle is…

teachBackPrompt: Cut a rectangle of paper along a diagonal to make two triangles, and show a grown-up why a triangle's area is HALF the rectangle's. Then measure a round plate's radius and estimate its area with π r².

revisionCard.summary: Rectangle: length × breadth. Parallelogram: base × height. Triangle: ½ × base × height (half a rectangle). Circle: π × radius × radius (π ≈ 3.14). Always use the STRAIGHT height, and give area in SQUARE units.

meta.curriculum: CBSE/NCERT Class 7 (area formulas — triangle, parallelogram, circle)
